findings.evidence: Mathlib uses this level for the same path-law projection: `Probability/Process/FiniteDimensionalLaws.lean:53-56` has `isProjectiveLimit_map (hX : AEMeasurable (fun ω ↦ (X · ω)) P)` and rewrites with `AEMeasurable.map_map_of_aemeasurable`; `Measure/Map.lean:90` defines `map` by a.e. measurability.
findings.fix: Restate with `hX : AEMeasurable (fun ω => fun i => X i ω) μ` (or derive that from `∀ i, AEMeasurable (X i) μ`) and prove via `AEMeasurable.map_map_of_aemeasurable`.
findings.issue: `map_prefixProj_pathLaw` assumes `hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)`, but the natural `Measure.map` composition hypothesis is a.e. measurability of the path map.

findings.evidence: `Basic.lean:135` only needs map composition; Mathlib’s corresponding finite-dimensional laws use `hX : ∀ t, AEMeasurable (X t) P` at `Probability/Process/FiniteDimensionalLaws.lean:43-49`.
findings.fix: Weaken `hXk` to `∀ i : Fin m, AEMeasurable (X (k i)) μ` (and `map_prefixLaw` to `∀ i : Fin n, AEMeasurable (X i.val) μ`), using `aemeasurable_pi_lambda` plus `AEMeasurable.map_map_of_aemeasurable`.
findings.issue: `map_blockLaw` and the derived `map_prefixLaw` require each selected coordinate to be `Measurable`, stronger than the a.e.-measurable hypotheses used by Mathlib’s finite-dimensional-law API.

summary: The definitions are at the roadmap level, but the map/composition lemmas use globally measurable process hypotheses where Mathlib’s process-law API works a.e. This makes the new API unnecessarily unusable for standard a.e.-measurable stochastic processes.
